edges.description: Impaired neural transmission disrupts synchronized brainstem responses despite preserved peripheral cochlear receptor activity.

Lesions affecting inner hair cells, ribbon synapses, spiral ganglion terminals, or auditory nerve fibers impair transmission of sound from the cochlea to the brain, producing the hearing-impairment spectrum.
intermediate_mechanisms: impaired transmission of sound from the cochlea to the brain

edges.description: Impaired synaptic release reduces temporal precision of auditory coding, which degrades speech discrimination.
Temporal coding impairment causes speech perception to be reduced out of proportion to hearing threshold elevation.
AIFM1-driven spiral ganglion neuronal injury can reduce temporal precision of auditory nerve discharge, impairing speech perception disproportionately to threshold elevation.
Reduced gap-junction communication in cochlear support cells can disrupt neural synchrony and degrade speech discrimination in TMEM43-associated auditory neuropathy.

nodes.description: AIFM1 variants can impair AIF dimerization and CHCHD4 interaction, producing mitochondrial dysfunction, mitochondrial calcium overload, ROS elevation, and caspase-independent apoptosis in patient-derived neurons.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired AIF-CHCHD4 interaction and mitochondrial calcium overload contribute to auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder in patient-iPSC-derived neurons with AIFM1 variant.
evidence.snippet: On the one hand, the mitochondrial import of ETC complex subunits was inhibited, subsequently leading to an increased ADP/ATP ratio and elevated ROS levels.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired AIF-CHCHD4 interaction and mitochondrial calcium overload contribute to auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder in patient-iPSC-derived neurons with AIFM1 variant.
evidence.snippet: Mitochondrial dysfunction, especially mCa2+ overload, plays a prominent role in ANSD associated with AIFM1.

nodes.description: Absent or markedly abnormal auditory brainstem responses are a core diagnostic hallmark of ANSD.

evidence.reference_title: Increased activity of Diaphanous homolog 3 (DIAPH3)/diaphanous causes hearing defects in humans with auditory neuropathy and in Drosophila.
evidence.snippet: Auditory neuropathy is a rare form of deafness characterized by an absent or abnormal auditory brainstem response with preservation of outer hair cell function.

evidence.reference_title: Cochlear Implantation in Children with Auditory Neuropathy: Meta-Analysis.
evidence.snippet: Its diagnosis is based on presence of otoacoustic emissions and lack of, or abnormal, brainstem auditory evoked potential.

nodes.description: Postsynaptic ANSD involves spiral ganglion terminals or auditory nerve fibers; impaired spike initiation, demyelination, axonal loss, or distal dendritic dysfunction reduce temporal precision of auditory nerve discharge.

evidence.reference_title: Information from cochlear potentials and genetic mutations helps localize the lesion site in auditory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: Abnormal discharge of auditory fibers results from lesions involving the nerve fibers themselves (postsynaptic AN), the inner hair cells (IHCs) or their synapses with auditory nerve terminals (presynaptic AN) [1].

evidence.reference_title: Information from cochlear potentials and genetic mutations helps localize the lesion site in auditory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: The demyelination of auditory nerve fibers observed in demyelinating neuropathies is expected to result in slowed conduction velocity with consequent disruption of temporal coding of acoustic signals (desynchrony)

nodes.description: Cochlear implantation is a major rehabilitative option for selected ANSD patients, with outcomes influenced by lesion site and cochlear nerve integrity.

evidence.reference_title: Outcomes of cochlear implantation in 75 patients with auditory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: CI is a viable therapy option for patients with AN; CN deficiency might impact outcomes of CI.

evidence.reference_title: Predictors of cochlear implant outcomes in pediatric auditory neuropathy: A matched case-control study.
evidence.snippet: Children with ANSD can achieve similar open-set speech perception outcomes as children with SNHL.

evidence.reference_title: Cochlear Implantation in Children with Auditory Neuropathy: Meta-Analysis.
evidence.snippet: CI provides the comparable benefit to children with ANSD in comparison to children with neurosensory hearing loss associated with other causes in their speech development.

nodes.description: Hearing aids may be trialed as part of hearing rehabilitation, but benefit is variable because ANSD primarily disrupts neural timing and speech perception.

evidence.reference_title: Current Advances in Gene Therapies of Genetic Auditory Neuropathy Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: To date, the only clinical treatment options for ANSD are hearing aids and cochlear implantation.

nodes.description: Hearing thresholds are variable, ranging from mild to profound impairment, and may improve, remain stable, fluctuate, or deteriorate depending on etiology and lesion site.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical and genetic architecture of a large cohort with auditory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: Auditory neuropathy (AN) is a unique type of language developmental disorder, with no precise rate of genetic contribution that has been deciphered in a large cohort.

evidence.reference_title: Current Advances in Gene Therapies of Genetic Auditory Neuropathy Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder (ANSD) refers to a range of hearing impairments characterized by an impaired transmission of sound from the cochlea to the brain.

nodes.description: ANSD is not a single lesion but a spectrum spanning inner hair cells, ribbon synapses, postsynaptic spiral ganglion terminals, and auditory nerve demyelination or axonal loss.

evidence.reference_title: Current Advances in Gene Therapies of Genetic Auditory Neuropathy Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: This defect can be due to a lesion or defect in the inner hair cell (IHC), IHC ribbon synapse (e.g., pre-synaptic release of glutamate), postsynaptic terminals of the spiral ganglion neurons, or demyelination and axonal loss within the auditory nerve.

nodes.description: Gene-replacement or gene-editing approaches for genetic ANSD, particularly OTOF-related presynaptic synaptopathy, remain emerging rather than standard clinical care.

evidence.reference_title: Current Advances in Gene Therapies of Genetic Auditory Neuropathy Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: genetic routes involving the replacement or corrective editing of mutant sequences or defected genes to repair damaged cells for the future restoration of hearing in deaf people are showing promise.

nodes.description: Speech perception is often disproportionately poor relative to pure-tone thresholds because temporal neural coding is impaired.

evidence.reference_title: Information from cochlear potentials and genetic mutations helps localize the lesion site in auditory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: The disruption of auditory nerve discharge underlies both the absence of or profound alterations in auditory brainstem responses and the severe impairment of speech perception.

evidence.reference_title: Information from cochlear potentials and genetic mutations helps localize the lesion site in auditory neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: some patients with AN may have normal hearing thresholds and an impairment of speech perception that is only apparent in the presence of noise

nodes.description: Biallelic OTOF variants impair otoferlin-dependent vesicle release and synapse maturation at inner hair cell ribbon synapses, producing a presynaptic auditory synaptopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Otoferlin Is Required for Proper Synapse Maturation and for Maintenance of Inner and Outer Hair Cells in Mouse Models for DFNB9.
evidence.snippet: We found otoferlin to be required for proper synapse development in the immature rodent cochlea: In absence of otoferlin, synaptic pruning was delayed, and postsynaptic boutons appeared enlarged at 2 weeks of age.

evidence.reference_title: Genetic analysis of auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder in the Korean population.
evidence.snippet: A novel nonsense mutation (p.Y1064X) and a known pathogenic mutation (p.R1939Q) of the OTOF gene were identified in a patient as compound heterozygote.

nodes.description: TMEM43 variants impair gap-junction intercellular communication in patient-derived iPSC glia-like support cells, reducing the cochlear support-cell coupling needed for temporally precise auditory signaling.

evidence.reference_title: Modeling of auditory neuropathy spectrum disorders associated with the TEME43 variant reveals impaired gap junction function of iPSC-derived glia-like support cells.
evidence.snippet: Reduced expression of genes associated with GLSs characteristics and reduced gap junction intercellular communication in TMEM43 mutant cell lines were observed compared to controls.
